hooks.prompt: Review this file change for security issues. Check for: hardcoded secrets/API keys, SQL injection, command injection via os/exec with user input, path traversal, use of unsafe package, weak crypto (MD5/SHA1 for security), unbounded allocations from external input, goroutine leaks (missing context cancellation), information disclosure in error messages. If ANY security issue is found, respond with decision:block and explain. Otherwise allow. Tool input: $ARGUMENTS

Check if this bash command is safe for a Go project. Block if it: deletes files outside the project, modifies system files, installs packages globally without user intent, runs curl/wget piped to shell, uses force flags on git destructively (push --force to main, reset --hard without cause), or could leak credentials. Allow standard Go toolchain commands, git read operations, and file inspection. Command: $ARGUMENTS

hooks.command: f=$(jq -r '.tool_response.filePath // .tool_input.file_path' 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$f" ] && echo "$f" | grep -qE '\.go$'; then gofmt -l "$f" 2>/dev/null | head -1 | { read -r line; if [ -n "$line" ]; then gofmt -w "$f" 2>/dev/null; echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PostToolUse","additionalContext":"Auto-formatted with gofmt"}}'; fi; }; fi
